_meta.description: Unified routing setup, workstreams, queues, classification, route-to-queue rules, assignment methods, and overflow behavior.

mcq.q: If Customer Service only is installed, what is true about unified routing by default?
options: It is enabled only for cases
It is enabled only after a queue is created
mcq.explanation: Microsoft states that if Customer Service only is installed, unified routing isn't enabled by default and must be enabled through the setup process.

wrongExplanations.0: That would remove the need for provisioning, which Microsoft explicitly documents.
wrongExplanations.1: It is not partially enabled for only one record type by default.
wrongExplanations.3: Queue creation does not automatically enable unified routing.

mcq.q: Where do administrators configure unified routing?
mcq.explanation: Unified routing is configured through the Copilot Service admin center app.

wrongExplanations.0: That center is important for environments, but not the documented routing configuration location.
wrongExplanations.2: Microsoft 365 admin center is not where unified routing is configured.
wrongExplanations.3: Runtime and admin configuration are not the same thing.

mcq.q: Which statement is true about Unified Service Desk and unified routing?
options: Unified routing requires Unified Service Desk
Unified routing is supported only for voice in Unified Service Desk
Unified routing isn't supported in Unified Service Desk
Unified Service Desk is required for preferred representative routing
mcq.explanation: Microsoft explicitly states that unified routing isn't supported in Unified Service Desk.

wrongExplanations.0: This is the opposite of the documentation.
wrongExplanations.1: There is no such supported voice-only exception in the cited article.
wrongExplanations.3: Preferred representative routing does not require Unified Service Desk.

mcq.q: For record routing, which app do representatives need to use to receive assignments through unified routing?
options: Any Dynamics 365 app with queues enabled
mcq.explanation: Representatives must use Copilot Service workspace to be assigned work through unified routing.

wrongExplanations.0: That is not the documented representative workspace.
wrongExplanations.1: Customer Insights is unrelated to receiving unified routing assignments.
wrongExplanations.3: Microsoft documents a specific required workspace, not any app.

mcq.q: In unified routing for records, what is a prerequisite before a table can be routed?
options: The table must be enabled for queues
The table must be an activity type
The table must have SLA enabled
The table must be owned by a team
mcq.explanation: Any record that you want to enable for routing needs to be enabled for queues first.

wrongExplanations.1: Records can be routed without being activity types, provided the table is queue-enabled.
wrongExplanations.2: SLA is not the prerequisite documented for queue routing.
wrongExplanations.3: Ownership model is separate from queue-enablement.

mcq.q: What happens to a work item if no route-to-queue rule matches and no rules are defined?
options: It is sent to the fallback queue of the workstream
It is assigned directly to the queue owner
mcq.explanation: If no queue routing rules are defined or no rules match, the incoming work item is routed to the fallback queue of the respective workstream.

wrongExplanations.0: Routing failure does not delete the work item.
wrongExplanations.2: The queue owner isn't the automatic fallback assignment target.
wrongExplanations.3: The documented behavior is fallback queue routing.

mcq.q: Which statement is true about workstreams in unified routing?
options: A single workstream can process any channel type at the same time
Workstreams are created for a specific channel scenario
Workstreams are optional when using advanced queues
mcq.explanation: Workstreams are created and managed for a specific channel scenario and are part of how unified routing organizes classification, route-to-queue, and assignment behavior.

wrongExplanations.0: Channel alignment matters in routing design.
wrongExplanations.2: Queues remain core routing containers.
wrongExplanations.3: Advanced queue routing relies on workstream-driven behavior.

mcq.q: Which queue-to-workstream alignment is valid in unified routing?
options: A voice workstream can route to a record queue
A record workstream can route to a voice queue
A messaging workstream should route to queues of the same channel type
Channel type alignment does not matter
mcq.explanation: Microsoft documents that queues and workstreams must align by channel type. A workstream should route to queues of the same relevant type.

wrongExplanations.0: This breaks channel-type alignment.
wrongExplanations.1: This also breaks channel-type alignment.
wrongExplanations.3: Channel type alignment matters in unified routing.

mcq.q: Which feature lets you define custom assignment behavior beyond the out-of-box assignment methods?
mcq.explanation: To use custom assignment methods, administrators create assignment rulesets comprising rules.

wrongExplanations.1: Preferred representatives are a separate routing capability.
wrongExplanations.2: Fallback queue is part of route-to-queue behavior, not custom assignment logic.
wrongExplanations.3: Queue privacy controls visibility, not assignment behavior.

mcq.q: What is one of the documented steps in creating assignment rulesets?
options: Configure conditions and order by options
Disable all built-in assignment methods first
Turn every queue into a private queue
Manually assign every representative a default case
mcq.explanation: When creating assignment rulesets, you configure conditions and an order-by option for the ruleset.

wrongExplanations.1: Built-in methods do not need to be disabled as a blanket prerequisite.
wrongExplanations.2: Queue privacy is unrelated to assignment ruleset creation.
wrongExplanations.3: That is not part of the documented process.

mcq.q: What business problem does preferred representative routing solve?
options: It forces all work to the queue owner
It routes work from a specific contact to the customer's preferred representative or relationship manager
It only works for bot conversations
mcq.explanation: Preferred representative routing supports a more personalized experience by routing work items from a specific contact to the customer's preferred representative or relationship manager.

wrongExplanations.0: Queue ownership isn't the purpose of preferred representative routing.
wrongExplanations.2: It does not replace all routing stages.
wrongExplanations.3: It is not restricted to bot conversations.

mcq.q: Which statement about preferred representative routing is true?
options: It applies only to Customer Service case information
It applies only to email activities
It can be used only with basic routing
mcq.explanation: Microsoft notes that the case information scenario in the preferred representative routing article is applicable to Customer Service only.

wrongExplanations.1: It is not limited to email activities.
wrongExplanations.2: Unified Service Desk is not required and is not supported for unified routing.
wrongExplanations.3: This is not documented as a basic-routing-only feature.

mcq.q: What is overflow in the context of unified routing queues?
options: A way to delete old items automatically
A queue behavior used when a queue reaches configured limits and work should be redirected or handled differently
A feature that changes table ownership
mcq.explanation: Overflow is used to manage work items when a queue reaches its configured limit or cannot continue receiving work under the defined conditions.

wrongExplanations.0: Overflow is not an automatic delete mechanism.
wrongExplanations.2: Fallback queue and overflow are related to routing but are not the same thing.
wrongExplanations.3: Overflow does not change ownership models.

mcq.q: Which setup area is directly associated with handling work items that exceed queue handling thresholds?
mcq.explanation: Overflow settings are used to define behavior when queue thresholds or limits are reached.

wrongExplanations.1: Skill types are part of skill-based routing, not queue overflow control.
wrongExplanations.2: Privacy affects visibility, not excess-work handling.
wrongExplanations.3: SLA KPIs are separate from unified routing overflow.

mcq.q: Which statement about email in unified routing is correct?
options: Emails can't be turned into work items
Emails are an example of an activity type that can be turned into work items and added to queues
Email routing works only with basic routing rulesets
Email routing does not use queues
mcq.explanation: Microsoft documents that emails are an example of activity records that can be turned into work items and added to queues for representatives to work on.

wrongExplanations.0: This directly contradicts the email routing article.
wrongExplanations.2: The article discusses unified routing for email records.
wrongExplanations.3: Queues are part of the documented process.

mcq.q: Which tool is used to set up custom flow to automatically route records?
mcq.explanation: The documented setup for automatically routing records uses a custom flow created in Power Automate.

wrongExplanations.0: Power BI is not used to create routing flows.
wrongExplanations.2: Azure DevOps Pipelines are unrelated to the documented runtime routing trigger setup.
wrongExplanations.3: Dataverse Search does not create routing triggers.

mcq.q: What is the first platform step in the documented custom-flow setup for automatic routing of records?
options: Open Power Automate in the correct environment and create an Automated cloud flow
Create a business rule on the Case table
mcq.explanation: The documented process begins in Power Automate by selecting the environment and creating an Automated cloud flow.

wrongExplanations.0: The article documents an automated cloud flow, not a desktop flow.
wrongExplanations.2: SLA enablement is not the first step in this flow setup.
wrongExplanations.3: A business rule is not the documented flow trigger approach.

mcq.q: Which statement is true about skill-based routing?
options: It uses skills to match incoming work with the best-suited representative
It works only for voice channels
It requires every queue to be public
mcq.explanation: Skill-based routing matches incoming work to the best-suited representative based on defined skills and related routing logic.

wrongExplanations.1: Queues remain part of routing.
wrongExplanations.2: It is not restricted to voice only.
wrongExplanations.3: Queue privacy is unrelated to whether skill-based routing can be used.

mcq.q: Before an administrator can create a skill such as Spanish, what must already exist?
options: A skill type such as Language
mcq.explanation: Microsoft states that you need at least one skill type in the system before you can create skills. For example, Language must exist before adding Spanish as a skill.

wrongExplanations.1: Queues are not the prerequisite for defining a skill.
wrongExplanations.2: SLA objects are unrelated to skill creation.
wrongExplanations.3: Private queue setup is not required for defining a skill.

mcq.q: What is used to define proficiency levels for skills?
mcq.explanation: Skill proficiency levels are defined through a rating model.

wrongExplanations.1: Overflow profiles are about queue load handling.
wrongExplanations.2: Queue counts do not define proficiency.
wrongExplanations.3: Case category is not a proficiency definition model.

mcq.q: By default, what is true about skill-based routing?
options: It is disabled until every queue is advanced
It works only after preferred representative routing is enabled
It is limited to chat records
mcq.explanation: Microsoft states that skill-based routing is enabled by default.

wrongExplanations.0: This is not the documented default state.
wrongExplanations.2: Preferred representative routing is separate.
wrongExplanations.3: It is not documented as being limited to chat only.

mcq.q: What must an administrator enable to let representatives add or remove skills at runtime for assigned work items?
mcq.explanation: To let representatives add or remove skills for assigned work items at runtime, the admin must enable the update skill control toggle in the Copilot Service admin center.

wrongExplanations.0: Automatic work distribution is about advanced queue behavior, not runtime skill editing.
wrongExplanations.2: Queue membership does not enable runtime skill updates.
wrongExplanations.3: Diagnostics do not enable runtime skill editing.

mcq.q: What is the purpose of Intelligent Skill Finder (ISF) in unified routing?
options: To configure machine learning-based skill classification rulesets for predicting skills
To replace queues with AI ranking
mcq.explanation: ISF is used to configure machine learning-based skill classification rulesets that predict skills.

wrongExplanations.0: ISF is not an archival feature.
wrongExplanations.2: It does not replace queues.
wrongExplanations.3: Manual skill assignment remains possible.

mcq.q: Why might an organization model existing tables as skills?
options: To avoid recreating data definitions as skills and avoid writing classification rules to stamp skills
To convert every record into an activity
To force all skills to be binary only
mcq.explanation: Microsoft says organizations can model existing tables as skills to avoid recreating data definitions as skills and avoid writing classification rules to stamp skills on incoming work items.

wrongExplanations.1: The feature does not disable tables.
wrongExplanations.2: It does not convert every record into an activity.
wrongExplanations.3: There is no such binary-only requirement.

mcq.q: Which statement about sentiment prediction for routing is correct?
options: It is a general availability feature meant for unrestricted production use
mcq.explanation: The sentiment prediction article explicitly marks the capability as a preview feature.

wrongExplanations.0: Microsoft explicitly says preview features aren't meant for production use.
wrongExplanations.2: The model looks at customer-provided messages, not representative chat strings.
wrongExplanations.3: Unified Service Desk is not the requirement here.

mcq.q: How many sentiment categories are on the documented sentiment prediction scale?
mcq.explanation: The sentiment model uses a seven-point scale: very positive, positive, slightly positive, neutral, slightly negative, negative, and very negative.

wrongExplanations.0: Three is too few for the documented model.
wrongExplanations.1: Five is not the documented count.
wrongExplanations.3: Ten is not the documented count.

mcq.q: What does the sentiment prediction model consider when classifying sentiment?
mcq.explanation: The documented model looks for and uses the messages provided by the customer and doesn't consider chat strings sent by the representative.

wrongExplanations.1: This is the opposite of the documented behavior.
wrongExplanations.2: Queue metadata is not the sentiment signal.
wrongExplanations.3: SLA warnings are not the model input.

mcq.q: When configuring the sentiment prediction model, how many input attributes can be defined?
mcq.explanation: You can define up to 10 attributes whose values can be used to build the prediction model.

wrongExplanations.0: The documented limit is higher.
wrongExplanations.1: The documented limit is higher.
wrongExplanations.3: There is a documented limit.

mcq.q: What is true about the first sentiment-model input attribute?
options: It is optional and can be deleted
It is mandatory and can't be deleted
It must always point to queue name
It can only be used for voice channels
mcq.explanation: At least one attribute is mandatory in the sentiment model setup, and the first one can't be deleted.

wrongExplanations.0: This contradicts the documented setup rule.
wrongExplanations.2: Queue name is not the required fixed first attribute.
wrongExplanations.3: The article does not impose a voice-only restriction here.

mcq.q: After enabling sentiment prediction, what rule type is used in work classification to apply it?
options: Machine learning model rule of type Sentiment prediction
mcq.explanation: In work classification, the sentiment model is configured as a machine learning model rule with type set to Sentiment prediction.

wrongExplanations.0: Prioritization is a different concern.
wrongExplanations.2: Overflow rules are queue handling rules.
wrongExplanations.3: Template rulesets are reusable templates, not the ML rule type itself.

mcq.q: What is stored as output from the sentiment prediction rule for use in later routing logic?
mcq.explanation: The output is stored in the Sentiment Category attribute and can be used in later rules.

wrongExplanations.0: Queue owner is not the ML output attribute.
wrongExplanations.2: Representative ID is not predicted by the sentiment rule.
wrongExplanations.3: Overflow reason is unrelated.

mcq.q: Which statement about basic case routing rulesets is correct?
options: Microsoft recommends them over unified routing for new record-routing design
Microsoft recommends enabling and using unified routing for automatically routing records
They are required before preferred representative routing can be used
They are the only supported way to route escalated cases
mcq.explanation: The basic routing rulesets article explicitly recommends enabling and using unified routing for automatically routing records in Customer Service.

wrongExplanations.0: This is the opposite of the recommendation.
wrongExplanations.2: Preferred representative routing is not documented as requiring legacy basic routing.
wrongExplanations.3: The article does not state that.

mcq.q: What is true about the old unified routing diagnostics feature in Dynamics 365 Customer Service?
options: It is the preferred long-term diagnostic tool
It is available only in Unified Service Desk
mcq.explanation: Microsoft marks the older unified routing diagnostics feature as deprecated.

wrongExplanations.0: That would contradict the deprecation notice.
wrongExplanations.2: This is not what the doc states.
wrongExplanations.3: Deprecation does not mean it replaces conversation diagnostics.

mcq.q: What are routing rules templates used for?
options: To reuse routing rulesets more easily across configuration scenarios
To replace skills with static queues
mcq.explanation: Routing rules templates are intended to help manage reusable ruleset patterns more efficiently.

wrongExplanations.1: Templates do not replace skills.
wrongExplanations.2: They are not analytics export features.
wrongExplanations.3: They do not bypass the routing pipeline.

mcq.q: Which statement best reflects Microsoft’s best-practices guidance for unified routing?
options: Skip design planning and fix routing once live traffic starts
Plan the setup carefully because routing design choices affect deployment and management quality
Use as many queues and rules as possible from day one
Always prefer private queues over public queues
mcq.explanation: The best-practices article emphasizes careful setup and design because deployment and management quality depend on these choices.

wrongExplanations.0: That is the opposite of best practice.
wrongExplanations.2: Quantity is not the same as sound routing design.
wrongExplanations.3: Queue privacy should follow operating need, not a blanket rule.

mcq.q: What audience does the unified routing FAQ explicitly mention?
mcq.explanation: The unified routing FAQ article explicitly says it contains questions administrators, supervisors, and representatives might have.

wrongExplanations.0: The article is broader than architects only.
wrongExplanations.2: It is not limited to developers.
wrongExplanations.3: Power BI analysts are not the named audience.

mcq.q: What is one documented business benefit of unified routing?
options: It removes the need for queues
It helps connect customers with the best-suited representative for the right first-contact outcome
It disables machine learning in routing
mcq.explanation: Microsoft lists connecting end customers with the best-suited representative to provide the right outcome on first contact as a key business benefit.

wrongExplanations.0: Queues remain part of the routing model.
wrongExplanations.2: Overflow can still exist and must be managed.
wrongExplanations.3: Machine learning is part of the documented capability set.

mcq.q: Which statement about machine learning in unified routing is correct?
options: ML can be used only for assignment, not classification
ML can be used in classification to add information to work items
ML is required for every workstream
ML can be used only when all queues are private
mcq.explanation: Microsoft states that rules and machine learning models can be used during classification to add information to work items.

wrongExplanations.0: ML is explicitly documented in classification.
wrongExplanations.2: ML is optional, not mandatory for every workstream.
wrongExplanations.3: Queue privacy is unrelated to whether ML can be used.

mcq.q: Which factor is part of assignment-stage decisioning in unified routing?
mcq.explanation: The assignment stage uses factors such as work-item needs, related entities, skills, and the current state of the workforce including availability and workload.

wrongExplanations.1: Browser version is not the routing decision factor described.
wrongExplanations.2: Dataverse storage capacity is not an assignment signal here.
wrongExplanations.3: Theme preference is irrelevant.

mcq.q: Unified routing in Dynamics 365 Customer Service consists of which two main stages?
mcq.explanation: Unified routing has two main stages: classification and assignment. In classification, rules or ML can add information such as skills, urgency, or category to the work item. In assignment, the item is prioritized and assigned to the best-suited representative based on attributes, skills, availability, and capacity.

wrongExplanations.0: Prioritization happens inside assignment logic, but it is not one of the two named core stages.
wrongExplanations.2: Queueing and resolution are business outcomes or downstream actions — they are not the two official stages of unified routing.
wrongExplanations.3: Skill detection and SLA enforcement are possible features around routing, but they are not the two official stage names.

mcq.q: Which application must representatives use in order to be assigned work through unified routing?
options: Any model-driven app with the Case table
mcq.explanation: Representatives must use Copilot Service workspace to be assigned work through unified routing. Microsoft explicitly documents this requirement and also notes that unified routing isn't supported in Unified Service Desk.

wrongExplanations.0: Customer Service Hub is not the required runtime app for receiving work via unified routing.
wrongExplanations.1: Unified Service Desk is explicitly not supported for unified routing.
wrongExplanations.3: Unified routing runtime assignment is not available in just any model-driven app.

mcq.q: When setting up unified routing for records, which action must happen first in the documented high-level sequence?
options: Provision unified routing in Service Configuration Settings
mcq.explanation: For record routing, Microsoft documents that the first step is to provision unified routing in Service Configuration Settings. After that come user setup, queue creation, record routing configuration, workstream setup, and diagnostics.

wrongExplanations.0: Creating a workstream comes later in the sequence, after provisioning and queue setup.
wrongExplanations.1: Routing diagnostics are enabled near the end of the record-routing setup flow, not first.
wrongExplanations.3: Assignment rules are configured after the queue and routing foundation are already in place.

mcq.q: How many route-to-queue rulesets can a single workstream have?
mcq.explanation: A workstream can have one route-to-queue ruleset only. That route-to-queue ruleset is evaluated after the work classification rulesets complete.

wrongExplanations.1: Microsoft does not allow two route-to-queue rulesets per workstream.
wrongExplanations.2: Five applies to the maximum number of queues in a percentage-based allocation rule, not the number of route-to-queue rulesets.
wrongExplanations.3: The number of queues does not change the one-ruleset-per-workstream limitation.

mcq.q: In the unified routing evaluation order, when is the route-to-queue ruleset processed?
mcq.explanation: The route-to-queue ruleset runs after all work classification rulesets. Classification first enriches or updates attributes on the work item, and then route-to-queue determines which queue receives it.

wrongExplanations.0: This is the reverse of the documented evaluation order.
wrongExplanations.2: Assignment rules are a different stage; route-to-queue does not wait for assignment failure.
wrongExplanations.3: Overflow handling affects queue choice behavior, but it does not determine whether route-to-queue runs.

mcq.q: What is the maximum percentage-based queue allocation that can be configured within a single route-to-queue rule?
mcq.explanation: Percentage-based routing can distribute work across up to five queues in a route-to-queue rule, and the total allocation across those queues must equal 100%.

wrongExplanations.0: The documented maximum is five queues, not three.
wrongExplanations.2: Ten queues is above the documented limit for percentage-based allocation in one rule.
wrongExplanations.3: The percentages must add up to 100, not 80.

mcq.q: Which statement is TRUE about work classification rulesets in a workstream?
options: You can create 5 rulesets per workstream, each with 50 rule items
You can create 10 rulesets per workstream, each with up to 100 rule items
You can create 100 rulesets per workstream, each with 10 rule items
There is no documented limit on rulesets or rule items
mcq.explanation: Microsoft documents that you can create 10 rulesets per workstream, and for each ruleset you can create up to 100 rule items.

wrongExplanations.0: Both the ruleset count and rule item count are understated here.
wrongExplanations.2: This reverses the documented numbers.
wrongExplanations.3: There are explicit documented limits.

mcq.q: What is the defining behavior of the Highest capacity assignment method?
options: It always assigns by earliest last-assignment timestamp only
It assigns to the representative with the highest available capacity, using round robin only to break ties
It ignores skills and presence and only checks capacity
It assigns items randomly among all queue members
mcq.explanation: Under Highest capacity, the system assigns the work item to the representative with the highest available capacity, provided they also meet the required skills and allowed presence conditions. If multiple representatives tie on capacity, round robin is used to break the tie.

wrongExplanations.0: Earliest last-assignment timestamp is part of the round-robin tie-break behavior, not the core definition of Highest capacity.
wrongExplanations.2: Highest capacity still respects required skills and allowed presence.
wrongExplanations.3: Assignment is not random.

mcq.q: What is the defining behavior of Advanced round robin assignment?
options: It always sends work to the newest user in the queue
It assigns based only on queue membership order and ignores capacity
It assigns to representatives who match skills, presence, and capacity, while maintaining a queue-wise round-robin order updated by assignments
It requires overflow to be enabled before it can function
mcq.explanation: In Advanced round robin, the system still checks skills, presence, and capacity, but maintains a queue-wise round-robin ordering that is updated as work gets assigned. This is intended to help distribute work more fairly.

wrongExplanations.0: It does not favor the newest queue member.
wrongExplanations.1: Capacity is still part of the matching criteria.
wrongExplanations.3: Overflow is a separate queue behavior and is not a prerequisite for round-robin assignment.

mcq.q: Which statement is TRUE about private queues in Dynamics 365 Customer Service?
options: Private queues prevent unauthorized users from accessing the underlying records automatically
Private queues are visible to the whole organization but hidden from agents
Private queues let a small group view and work queue items, but they do not by themselves secure the underlying records
Private queues are only available for advanced queues with unified routing
mcq.explanation: A private queue is used to let a small group of members view and work queue items, helping reduce clutter in other users' views. However, private queues do not restrict access to the records they contain; record security must still be handled through the security model.

wrongExplanations.0: This is the classic trap. Private queue visibility is not the same thing as record-level security.
wrongExplanations.1: That describes neither public nor private queue behavior correctly.
wrongExplanations.3: Private/public queue type is documented on basic queues as well; it is not limited to advanced queues.

mcq.q: What happens after you convert a basic queue to an advanced queue by setting Automatic work distribution to Yes?
options: You can switch it back to a basic queue at any time
You must disable unified routing on the workstream first to revert it
You cannot revert it back to a basic queue; you must create a new basic queue instead
mcq.explanation: Microsoft states that once a basic queue is updated to an advanced queue by enabling Automatic work distribution, you can't revert it back to a basic queue. If you need a basic queue again, you must create a new one.

wrongExplanations.0: This is explicitly not supported.
wrongExplanations.1: There is no supported rollback path through the workstream.
wrongExplanations.3: Queue privacy is a separate setting and is not changed automatically by enabling advanced queue behavior.

dragdrop.instruction: Match each setup area to its purpose.
zones: Turns on unified routing capability when it isn't enabled by default
Makes a record type eligible for routing through queues
Defines channel-specific routing container and behavior
Sends work items to the right queue or fallback queue
Defines custom assignment logic beyond out-of-box methods

correctMapping.Provision unified routing: Turns on unified routing capability when it isn't enabled by default
correctMapping.Enable table for queues: Makes a record type eligible for routing through queues
correctMapping.Create workstream: Defines channel-specific routing container and behavior
correctMapping.Configure route-to-queue rules: Sends work items to the right queue or fallback queue
correctMapping.Configure assignment rulesets: Defines custom assignment logic beyond out-of-box methods

dragdrop.explanation: Provisioning enables the capability, table queue-enablement makes records routable, the workstream defines the channel routing context, route-to-queue picks the queue, and assignment rulesets control custom assignment behavior.

dragdrop.instruction: Match each skill-routing concept to the correct description.
zones: Parent classification needed before creating a specific capability like Spanish
A specific capability used in matching work
Defines proficiency levels
Lets representatives add or remove skills at runtime for assigned work items
Uses machine learning to predict skills

correctMapping.Skill type: Parent classification needed before creating a specific capability like Spanish
correctMapping.Skill: A specific capability used in matching work
correctMapping.Update skill control: Lets representatives add or remove skills at runtime for assigned work items
correctMapping.Intelligent Skill Finder: Uses machine learning to predict skills

dragdrop.explanation: Skill setup starts with skill types, specific skills are created under them, proficiency is modeled with a rating model, runtime editing uses the update skill control, and ISF predicts skills via machine learning.

dragdrop.instruction: Match each sentiment-routing element to its correct meaning.
zones: Not intended for unrestricted production use
The number of documented sentiment classifications
The input source the model evaluates
The output attribute used in later routing logic
An option to test phrases and inspect score, category, and keywords

correctMapping.Preview feature: Not intended for unrestricted production use
correctMapping.Seven-point scale: The number of documented sentiment classifications
correctMapping.Customer messages: The input source the model evaluates
correctMapping.Sentiment Category: The output attribute used in later routing logic
correctMapping.Dry run: An option to test phrases and inspect score, category, and keywords

dragdrop.explanation: Sentiment prediction is preview, uses a seven-point classification scale, looks at customer messages, stores output in Sentiment Category, and can be tested with Dry run.

dragdrop.instruction: Match each queue or routing concept to the correct description.
zones: Destination used when no route-to-queue rule matches
Visible to a small group of members for working queue items
Visible across the organization
Behavior used when queue limits or thresholds are reached
Routes work from a contact toward a preferred representative or relationship manager

correctMapping.Fallback queue: Destination used when no route-to-queue rule matches
correctMapping.Private queue: Visible to a small group of members for working queue items
correctMapping.Overflow: Behavior used when queue limits or thresholds are reached
correctMapping.Preferred representative routing: Routes work from a contact toward a preferred representative or relationship manager

dragdrop.explanation: These are distinct queue-related behaviors: fallback handles unmatched items, private/public control queue visibility, overflow handles threshold conditions, and preferred representative routing supports personalized assignment.

dragdrop.instruction: Match each routing stage or capability to its correct role.
zones: Adds or derives information on work items before queue/rep decisions
Prioritizes and allocates work to representatives
Can enrich work items with predictive signals
Legacy/older approach that Microsoft says unified routing is recommended over for automatic record routing
A role-based reference for admins, supervisors, and representatives

correctMapping.Classification: Adds or derives information on work items before queue/rep decisions
correctMapping.Assignment: Prioritizes and allocates work to representatives
correctMapping.Machine learning in classification: Can enrich work items with predictive signals
correctMapping.Basic routing rulesets: Legacy/older approach that Microsoft says unified routing is recommended over for automatic record routing
correctMapping.Unified routing FAQ: A role-based reference for admins, supervisors, and representatives

dragdrop.explanation: Classification precedes assignment, machine learning can enrich work items during classification, Microsoft recommends unified routing over legacy basic routing for automatic record routing, and the FAQ is written for multiple role audiences.

dragdrop.instruction: Match each artifact or feature to whether it is active guidance or flagged with a limitation/deprecation note.
zones: Deprecated feature
Preview feature
Required representative workspace for receiving assignments
Prerequisite for routing a record type
Runtime skill editing toggle

correctMapping.Copilot Service workspace: Required representative workspace for receiving assignments
correctMapping.Queue-enabled table: Prerequisite for routing a record type

dragdrop.explanation: This separates what is current guidance from what has a note attached: diagnostics is deprecated, sentiment prediction is preview, Copilot Service workspace is required for assignments, queue-enabled tables are prerequisite for record routing, and update skill control enables runtime skill changes.

dragdrop.instruction: Match each unified routing concept to its correct description.
zones: Adds or derives information on a work item using rules or ML before assignment
Prioritizes and allocates work to the best-suited representative based on requirements and workforce state
Determines which queue gets the work item after classification completes
Chooses the representative with the greatest available capacity, using round robin to break ties
Distributes work fairly across matching representatives using queue-wise round-robin order

correctMapping.Classification: Adds or derives information on a work item using rules or ML before assignment
correctMapping.Assignment: Prioritizes and allocates work to the best-suited representative based on requirements and workforce state
correctMapping.Route-to-queue ruleset: Determines which queue gets the work item after classification completes
correctMapping.Highest capacity: Chooses the representative with the greatest available capacity, using round robin to break ties
correctMapping.Advanced round robin: Distributes work fairly across matching representatives using queue-wise round-robin order

dragdrop.explanation: Classification enriches the work item first, then route-to-queue places it into the correct queue, and assignment allocates it to a representative. Highest capacity and Advanced round robin are two distinct assignment methods with different distribution behavior.

dragdrop.instruction: Match each queue type or setting to the correct meaning.
zones: Everyone in the organization can view the queue and its work items
A small group of members can view and work queue items
Creates a basic queue
Creates an advanced queue for unified routing
Controls access to the underlying records, not queue visibility alone

correctMapping.Public queue: Everyone in the organization can view the queue and its work items
correctMapping.Private queue: A small group of members can view and work queue items
correctMapping.Automatic work distribution = Yes: Creates an advanced queue for unified routing
correctMapping.Security model: Controls access to the underlying records, not queue visibility alone

dragdrop.explanation: Public and private describe who can see and work queue items. Automatic work distribution determines whether the queue is basic or advanced for unified routing. Record access is governed separately by the security model.

dragdrop.instruction: Match each route-to-queue behavior or limit to its correct rule.
zones: A workstream supports only this many route-to-queue rulesets
Default policy that evaluates all rule conditions and considers overflow handling
Stops evaluation after the first matching rule is found
Can distribute work across up to five queues totaling 100%
Can be created up to 10 per workstream, with up to 100 rule items in each

correctMapping.One route-to-queue ruleset: A workstream supports only this many route-to-queue rulesets
correctMapping.Hit-all: Default policy that evaluates all rule conditions and considers overflow handling
correctMapping.Hit-first: Stops evaluation after the first matching rule is found
correctMapping.Percentage-based allocation: Can distribute work across up to five queues totaling 100%
correctMapping.Work classification rulesets: Can be created up to 10 per workstream, with up to 100 rule items in each

dragdrop.explanation: A workstream gets only one route-to-queue ruleset. Hit-all is the default route evaluation policy, Hit-first stops at the first match, percentage allocation supports up to five queues summing to 100%, and work classification rulesets have documented limits per workstream.

casestudy.title: Northwind Record Routing Foundation
casestudy.scenario: Northwind is moving from manual record triage to unified routing in Dynamics 365 Customer Service. The admin team has not yet enabled unified routing. They want to route cases and custom complaint records automatically. Premium complaints must go through a dedicated routing pipeline, but the custom complaint table is not yet queue-enabled. The team also wants custom assignment behavior for specialist queues and wants representatives to receive assigned work in the correct workspace.

msRef.label: Set up unified routing for records

questions.q: What should the team do first before building the rest of the record-routing solution?
questions.explanation: Provisioning unified routing is the first foundational step when the capability isn't already enabled.

wrongExplanations.0: Queues come later in the setup flow.
wrongExplanations.2: That is optional and unrelated to first-time enablement.
wrongExplanations.3: Queue type choice is not the first foundational action.

questions.q: What must be true before the custom complaint table can be routed through unified routing?
options: It must be an activity table only
questions.explanation: Records must be enabled for queues before they can participate in unified routing.

wrongExplanations.1: The prerequisite is queue enablement, not activity-only classification.
wrongExplanations.2: Privacy is a queue setting, not a table prerequisite.
wrongExplanations.3: Overflow is optional queue behavior, not a table prerequisite.

questions.q: Northwind wants custom assignment behavior beyond the built-in queue assignment methods. What should they configure?
questions.explanation: Custom assignment logic is configured through assignment rulesets.

wrongExplanations.1: Fallback queue is about queue selection when no rule matches.
wrongExplanations.2: Microsoft recommends unified routing for automatic record routing.
wrongExplanations.3: Diagnostics do not control assignment logic.

questions.q: Which workspace must representatives use to receive work assigned through unified routing?
questions.explanation: Representatives must use Copilot Service workspace for assignments through unified routing.

wrongExplanations.0: Unified Service Desk is not supported for unified routing.
wrongExplanations.1: Customer Insights is not the assignment workspace.
wrongExplanations.3: Microsoft specifies a particular workspace, not any app.

questions.q: If Northwind defines no route-to-queue rule for some complaint records, where will those records go?
options: They go to the fallback queue for the workstream
They are assigned directly to the queue owner
questions.explanation: When no route-to-queue rule matches, the work item is routed to the fallback queue.

wrongExplanations.0: The documented fallback behavior prevents this.
wrongExplanations.2: Queue owners are not the documented fallback destination.
wrongExplanations.3: Routing does not auto-close the record.

casestudy.title: Fabrikam Skill and Sentiment Rollout
casestudy.scenario: Fabrikam wants to improve first-contact resolution by using skill-based routing and sentiment-based classification for incoming support work. The admin wants to create skills such as Spanish and Premium Product Support, let representatives adjust skills at runtime for assigned work, and use a machine-learning model to identify negative sentiment from customer-submitted text. The team also wants to use existing product tables as skills instead of recreating everything manually.

msRef.label: Set up skill-based routing in unified routing

questions.q: Before creating the skill 'Spanish', what must the admin create first?
options: A skill type such as Language
questions.explanation: A skill type must exist before you can create a specific skill such as Spanish.

wrongExplanations.0: Queue creation is not the prerequisite.
wrongExplanations.2: Sentiment categories are unrelated here.
wrongExplanations.3: Preferred representative routing is separate.

questions.q: What must the admin enable to let representatives add or remove skills during work handling?
questions.explanation: The update skill control toggle lets representatives add or remove skills at runtime for assigned work items.

wrongExplanations.0: Overflow handles queue capacity situations.
wrongExplanations.2: Basic routing rulesets are unrelated.
wrongExplanations.3: Queue privacy does not control runtime skill editing.

questions.q: Which statement about sentiment prediction is correct for Fabrikam's design?
options: It is a preview feature that can classify customer sentiment on a seven-point scale
It can be used only for voice conversations
questions.explanation: Sentiment prediction is preview and classifies customer sentiment on a seven-point scale.

wrongExplanations.0: It evaluates customer-provided text, not representative chat strings.
wrongExplanations.2: The article does not limit it to voice only.
wrongExplanations.3: It feeds routing logic; it does not replace the routing pipeline.

questions.q: Why might Fabrikam model existing product tables as skills?
options: To avoid recreating those data definitions as new skill records and avoid extra classification rules
To force all queues to be public
To remove the need for rating models
questions.explanation: Modeling existing tables as skills helps avoid recreating data definitions and can remove the need to write classification rules to stamp skills.

wrongExplanations.1: The feature does not disable ML.
wrongExplanations.2: Queue privacy is unrelated.
wrongExplanations.3: Rating models remain relevant for skill proficiency.

questions.q: What is the role of Intelligent Skill Finder in this scenario?
options: It configures machine-learning-based skill prediction rulesets
questions.explanation: ISF is used to configure machine-learning-based skill classification rulesets to predict skills.

wrongExplanations.0: That is not its purpose.
wrongExplanations.2: Manual skill definition remains possible.
wrongExplanations.3: Queues remain part of the routing model.

casestudy.title: Litware Queue Operations and Personalization
casestudy.scenario: Litware runs multiple support teams. Standard support goes to shared public operational queues. VIP account customers should be routed toward specific relationship managers when possible. HR and legal complaint items should be visible only to a small specialist group, but security must still protect the underlying records. Queue spikes are common, so the team wants documented handling for when queues hit configured thresholds.

msRef.label: Create and manage queues for unified routing

questions.q: Which queue type best fits Litware's shared standard support queue that many people across the organization need to see?
questions.explanation: Public queues are visible across the organization and fit broad operational visibility needs.

wrongExplanations.1: Private queues are for smaller member groups.
wrongExplanations.2: Fallback queue is a routing outcome, not a visibility type.
wrongExplanations.3: Overflow is a behavior, not a queue type name here.

questions.q: Which feature best fits the VIP account requirement to route work from a contact toward a specific relationship manager?
questions.explanation: Preferred representative routing is designed for routing work from a contact toward the customer's preferred representative or relationship manager.

wrongExplanations.1: This is not the personalization feature for preferred reps.
wrongExplanations.2: Fallback queue is for unmatched routing.
wrongExplanations.3: Update skill control is unrelated.

questions.q: Which statement is correct about Litware using a private queue for HR/legal complaints?
options: The private queue alone secures the underlying records
The private queue limits who sees queue items, but record security must still be handled separately
Private queues cannot be used for specialist teams
Private queues are available only in legacy basic routing
questions.explanation: Private queue membership affects queue-item visibility, but record access must still be enforced through the security model.

wrongExplanations.0: This confuses visibility with security.
wrongExplanations.2: Specialist teams are a valid private-queue use case.
wrongExplanations.3: Private queues are not limited that way.

questions.q: Which configuration area should Litware use for handling queue spikes when thresholds are reached?
questions.explanation: Overflow handles queue situations where configured thresholds or limits are reached.

wrongExplanations.1: Rating models are for skill proficiency.
wrongExplanations.2: Sentiment prediction is classification logic, not threshold handling.
wrongExplanations.3: Template rulesets are for reuse patterns.

questions.q: If Litware later enables Automatic work distribution on an existing basic queue, what is true?
options: It can be reverted back to basic at any time
The queue becomes an advanced queue and cannot be reverted to basic
questions.explanation: When Automatic work distribution is enabled, the queue becomes advanced and cannot be reverted back to a basic queue.

wrongExplanations.0: Microsoft explicitly says it can't be reverted.
wrongExplanations.2: Privacy is a separate setting.
wrongExplanations.3: Preferred representative routing is not disabled by that change.

casestudy.title: Adventure Works Governance and Troubleshooting
casestudy.scenario: Adventure Works wants a routing rollout that is maintainable over time. The team is debating whether to keep building on legacy basic routing rulesets or move fully to unified routing. They also want reusable routing patterns, role-friendly reference material for admins and supervisors, and clarity on which troubleshooting tools are current versus deprecated.

msRef.label: Use best practices to set up unified routing in Customer Service

questions.q: What does Microsoft recommend for automatically routing records in Customer Service?
options: Use basic routing rulesets instead of unified routing
Use no routing rules at all
questions.explanation: Microsoft recommends enabling and using unified routing for automatically routing records in Customer Service.

wrongExplanations.0: This is opposite to the recommendation.
wrongExplanations.2: That would not meet automated routing goals.
wrongExplanations.3: Manual assignment is not the recommended automation path.

questions.q: Which capability helps Adventure Works reuse routing logic patterns more efficiently?
questions.explanation: Routing rules templates are intended to support reuse and easier management of ruleset patterns.

wrongExplanations.1: Privacy affects who sees queue items, not reuse of routing patterns.
wrongExplanations.2: Overflow handles queue thresholds.
wrongExplanations.3: Unified Service Desk is not the answer here.

questions.q: Which document is explicitly positioned as a role-friendly reference for administrators, supervisors, and representatives?
questions.explanation: The unified routing FAQ explicitly names administrators, supervisors, and representatives as its audience.

wrongExplanations.1: That is not the named audience doc here.
wrongExplanations.2: This is unrelated to routing FAQ audience.
wrongExplanations.3: API limits are a different topic.

questions.q: Which statement about the older unified routing diagnostics feature is correct?
options: It is the only supported diagnostics path
It is mandatory for assignment rulesets
questions.explanation: The old unified routing diagnostics article is explicitly marked deprecated.

wrongExplanations.1: That would contradict the deprecation note.
wrongExplanations.2: It is not a prerequisite for assignment rulesets.
wrongExplanations.3: Diagnostics and templates are different capabilities.

questions.q: What is the best reading of Microsoft's best-practice stance for routing design?
options: Treat routing design casually because it can always be cleaned up with no impact
Careful setup and planning matter because routing design choices affect successful deployment and management
Always create the maximum number of rulesets available
Skip queue strategy if skill-based routing is enabled
questions.explanation: Microsoft's best-practices article stresses careful setup and design as key to successful deployment and management.

wrongExplanations.0: That is the opposite of best practice.
wrongExplanations.2: Using maximum counts is not itself a best practice.
wrongExplanations.3: Queue strategy still matters even with skills.

casestudy.title: Contoso Unified Routing Rollout
casestudy.scenario: Contoso is implementing unified routing for record-based support in Dynamics 365 Customer Service. The admin team has the following requirements: 1. Unified routing must be enabled correctly before record routing goes live 2. Cases should first be enriched with routing attributes such as priority and product line 3. After enrichment, cases must be routed to the correct queue 4. Premium customers should be distributed between two BPO vendor queues using percentage-based allocation 5. The support lead wants fair work distribution across equally qualified agents 6. HR complaint cases must only be visible to a small specialist team, but the organization's security model must still protect the actual records The team is deciding how to configure provisioning, classification, queues, route-to-queue rules, and assignment methods.

questions.q: Before configuring queues, workstreams, and diagnostics for record routing, what should the administrator do first?
options: Provision unified routing in Service Configuration Settings
questions.explanation: For records, Microsoft documents that the first step is to provision unified routing in Service Configuration Settings. This comes before queue creation, record routing setup, workstreams, and diagnostics.

wrongExplanations.0: Workstream creation comes later in the documented setup sequence.
wrongExplanations.2: Diagnostics are enabled near the end of the flow, not at the start.
wrongExplanations.3: Assignment rules are configured after the core routing foundation exists.

questions.q: Contoso wants to add priority and product-line information to a case before deciding its queue. Which routing stage should do this work?
questions.explanation: This belongs in classification. Classification rules or ML models add or derive information on the work item before the route-to-queue and assignment logic runs.

wrongExplanations.0: Assignment happens after classification and focuses on choosing the representative.
wrongExplanations.2: Overflow handles what happens when a queue can't take more work — it does not enrich the work item.
wrongExplanations.3: Queue creation defines containers and members, not runtime enrichment logic.

questions.q: Contoso wants premium cases split between Vendor Queue A and Vendor Queue B in a fixed ratio. What is the correct route-to-queue design?
options: Create two separate route-to-queue rulesets in the same workstream
Use percentage-based allocation in a single route-to-queue rule, with allocations totaling 100%
Use Advanced round robin at the queue stage
Use private queues because private queues can split traffic automatically
questions.explanation: The correct design is percentage-based allocation inside a route-to-queue rule. Microsoft allows up to five queues in this configuration, and the allocation across them must total 100%.

wrongExplanations.0: A workstream can only have one route-to-queue ruleset.
wrongExplanations.2: Advanced round robin is an assignment method for representatives, not a queue-splitting method.
wrongExplanations.3: Private queue visibility has nothing to do with traffic splitting.

questions.q: HR complaint cases should be worked by a small specialist team only, and the architect claims that using a private queue alone will secure the case data. What is the best response?
options: Correct — private queues secure the underlying records automatically
Incorrect — private queues limit who sees queue items, but record security must still be enforced through the security model
Incorrect — only public queues are supported for specialist work
Correct — private queues and advanced round robin together secure the records
questions.explanation: That claim is incorrect. Private queues help restrict who sees and works the queue items, but they do not secure the underlying records. Record-level access must still be enforced through the Dynamics 365 security model.

wrongExplanations.0: This confuses queue visibility with record security.
wrongExplanations.2: Private queues are explicitly supported for small groups.
wrongExplanations.3: Advanced round robin affects assignment fairness, not record security.

questions.q: The support lead wants fair distribution across equally qualified agents while still considering skills, presence, and capacity. Which assignment method best fits?
questions.explanation: Advanced round robin is the best fit when the goal is fairer distribution across equally qualified agents while still matching on skills, presence, and capacity. Hit-first and Hit-all are route-to-queue policies, not representative assignment methods.

wrongExplanations.0: Highest capacity is more capacity-driven and uses round robin mainly as a tie-breaker.
wrongExplanations.2: Hit-first is a route-to-queue rule-hit policy, not an assignment method for representatives.
wrongExplanations.3: Hit-all is also a route-to-queue rule-hit policy.
